V600.title: Rate Limits for the REST API — GitHub Docs
V600.author: GitHub
V600.excerpt: If you use a personal access token, a GitHub App, or an OAuth app, you can make up to 5,000 requests per hour. Unauthenticated requests are limited to 60 requests per hour, associated with the originating IP address.
V600.relevance: Primary source for GitHub REST API rate limits

V601.title: Rate Limits and Query Limits for the GraphQL API — GitHub Docs
V601.author: GitHub
V601.excerpt: For users, the limit is 5,000 points per hour, including requests made with personal access tokens as well as requests made by GitHub Apps or OAuth apps on behalf of authorized users. For OAuth apps, the limit is 5,000 points per hour, or 10,000 points per hour if the app is owned by a GitHub Enterprise Cloud organization.
V601.relevance: GitHub GraphQL API rate limits for authenticated requests

V602.title: GitHub Terms of Service — GitHub Docs
V602.author: GitHub
V602.excerpt: You may not use information from the Service (whether scraped, collected through the API, or obtained otherwise) for spamming purposes, including for the purposes of sending unsolicited emails to users or selling personal information, such as to recruiters, headhunters, and job boards.
V602.relevance: Key ToS clause prohibiting selling GitHub user data to recruiters

V603.title: GitHub Acceptable Use Policies — GitHub Docs
V603.author: GitHub
V603.excerpt: Scraping refers to extracting data from GitHub via an automated process, such as a bot or webcrawler, and does not refer to the collection of information through GitHub's API. You may not scrape GitHub for spamming purposes, including for the purposes of selling GitHub users' personal information.
V603.relevance: GitHub policy on scraping vs. API distinction; recruitment prohibition

V604.title: X API Rate Limits — X Developer Documentation
V604.author: X Corp
V604.excerpt: Requests using OAuth can make up to 10,000 requests per 15 minutes per app (Bearer Token) for /2/users/:id/tweets endpoint. Per user (OAuth 1.0a or OAuth 2.0 user tokens): 900 requests per 15 minutes.
V604.relevance: Official X API rate limits for user timeline and profile reads

V605.title: X (Twitter) API Pricing in 2026: All Tiers — Postproxy
V605.author: Postproxy
V605.excerpt: As of February 2026, pay-per-use is the default X API pricing for new developers at $0.01 per post created and $0.005 per post read, capped at 2 million reads/month, with the free tier discontinued. Legacy Basic ($200/month) and Pro ($5,000/month) tiers remain only for existing subscribers.
V605.relevance: X API pricing tiers as of 2026, including pay-per-use model
V605.credibility: medium — third-party documentation site; consistent with other sources

V606.title: Court Says Twitter Misused Litigation to Punish Defendants for Their Speech — X v. CCDH
V606.author: Eric Goldman
V606.excerpt: Judge Breyer stated: 'This case is about punishing the Defendants for their speech…X Corp. has brought this case in order to punish CCDH for CCDH publications that criticized X Corp.' The judge noted there was no allegation that CCDH scraped anything other than public tweets, and that no private user information was involved.
V606.relevance: X v. CCDH 2024 ruling: X lost; court found ToS-based scraping claims were pretextual
V606.credibility: high — legal scholar analysis of court ruling; cites primary sources

V607.title: OAuth 2.0 — Twitter Developer Platform
V607.author: X Corp / Twitter
V607.excerpt: OAuth 2.0 is the modern standard for authentication and is recommended for all new development. tweet.read scope is required even when accessing basic user profile data via /users/me endpoint. Default user object returns only id, name, and username fields.
V607.relevance: X OAuth 2.0 scopes for reading user profile and tweet data

V608.title: Getting Access to LinkedIn APIs — LinkedIn/Microsoft Learn
V608.author: LinkedIn/Microsoft
V608.excerpt: Most permissions and partner programs require explicit approval from LinkedIn. Open Permissions are the only permissions that are available to all developers without special approval. LinkedIn deprecated the classic r_liteprofile and r_emailaddress scopes in 2026. All new apps must use OpenID Connect scopes instead.
V608.relevance: LinkedIn API access restrictions, scope deprecations, approval requirements

V609.title: LinkedIn API Terms of Use
V609.author: LinkedIn
V609.excerpt: LinkedIn prohibits selling access to an aggregated collection of Member profiles, the most relevant Members for a position, or any social activity such as posts, likes, or shares by Members. LinkedIn explicitly restricts using APIs to retrieve content that is then aggregated with third party data in such a way that a User cannot attribute the Content to LinkedIn.
V609.relevance: LinkedIn's explicit ToS prohibition on profile aggregation for third-party talent products

V610.title: hiQ v. LinkedIn Wrapped Up: Web Scraping Lessons Learned — ZwillGen
V610.author: ZwillGen LLP
V610.excerpt: LinkedIn and hiQ Labs agreed to a consent judgment and permanent injunction to resolve all data scraping related claims after six years of litigation. The stipulation includes a $500,000 judgment entered against hiQ, establishment of hiQ's liability under California common law torts of trespass to chattels and misappropriation, and various forms of injunctive relief effectively prohibiting hiQ's future ability to data scrape LinkedIn.
V610.relevance: hiQ v. LinkedIn 2022 settlement: $500K judgment; scraping LinkedIn via state torts, not CFAA
V610.credibility: high — law firm analysis of actual court settlement; cites filed documents

V611.title: Is LinkedIn Automation Safe in 2026? ToS, Scraping Rules & What's Actually Allowed
V611.author: ConnectSafely
V611.excerpt: In January 2025, LinkedIn filed suit against Nubela (the company behind Proxycurl, a popular LinkedIn data API), and in early 2025 both Apollo.io and Seamless.ai had their company pages removed from LinkedIn for terms-of-service violations.
V611.relevance: LinkedIn's 2025 active enforcement against profile aggregation services (Proxycurl, Apollo.io)
V611.credibility: medium — technology/legal guidance site; cites specific recent enforcement actions

V612.title: Best Substack Newsletter Data APIs for Developers (2026) — Netrows
V612.author: Netrows
V612.excerpt: Substack does not offer a public API for developers, and there is no official way to programmatically access author profiles, newsletter posts, or comments. Every Substack newsletter automatically has a public RSS feed at newsletter.substack.com/feed. The RSS feeds have problems including that they don't have the full content for paywalled posts.
V612.relevance: Confirms Substack has no official API; only RSS available for public posts
V612.credibility: medium — developer-focused blog; consistent with observed platform behavior

V613.title: How to Scrape Substack Newsletters in 2026: A Complete Guide
V613.author: The Data Collector
V613.excerpt: Substack's TOS prohibits 'copying' and 'scraping' data, but it's unclear if that applies to accessing metadata through its undocumented API endpoints. Accessing platform content programmatically, especially at scale, could potentially violate Substack's terms of use.
V613.relevance: Substack ToS prohibition on scraping; gray zone for undocumented API endpoints
V613.credibility: medium — practitioner analysis; ToS assessment is accurate

V614.title: How to Export Substack Subscribers — Grow Your Newsletter
V614.author: Grow Your Newsletter
V614.excerpt: None of this information is available through Substack's official data export. You can download your own posts, comments, and subscribers, but not a list of the newsletters you subscribe to, your engagement history, or what shows up on your Substack feed.
V614.relevance: What Substack's official user data export includes and excludes
V614.credibility: medium — newsletter practitioner; describes actual export behavior

V615.title: How to Obtain Behance API Access — Adobe Experience League Community
V615.author: Adobe Community
V615.excerpt: The Behance API has been undergoing a technical migration and was unavailable, with Adobe hoping to make it available on adobe.io in the future but having no specific date to share. This indicates that the Behance API status remains transitional.
V615.relevance: Current Behance API status: transitional migration to adobe.io; approval required
V615.credibility: high — Adobe's own community forum; reflects actual Adobe support guidance

V616.title: How to Use the Behance API to Build a Custom Portfolio Web Page — Envato Tuts+
V616.author: Envato Tuts+
V616.excerpt: Behance's overall goal is 'to provide just about every function of Behance through the API, both read and write.' The API provides granular access to projects, users and works in progress by searching a number of different criteria such as popularity, tags, location, creative fields, and more.
V616.relevance: What the Behance API was designed to provide; endpoint coverage
V616.credibility: medium — third-party developer tutorial; describes original API capabilities

V617.title: Overview for the v2 API — Dribbble API
V617.author: Dribbble
V617.excerpt: The Dribbble API is accessed over HTTPS from the api.dribbble.com/v2/ endpoint and data is sent and received as JSON. Rate limits: 60 requests per minute and 1,440 requests per day per authenticated user. Pagination: 30 items default, up to 100 per page.
V617.relevance: Official Dribbble API v2 documentation: rate limits, endpoints, data format

V618.title: OAuth — Dribbble API
V618.author: Dribbble
V618.excerpt: Developers must authenticate using OAuth. OAuth2 is a protocol that lets external applications request authorization to private details in a user's Dribbble account without getting their password. Scopes limit access for OAuth tokens. They do not grant any additional permission beyond that which the user already has.
V618.relevance: Dribbble OAuth 2.0 scopes for reading portfolio data

V619.title: Terms — Dribbble API
V619.author: Dribbble
V619.excerpt: Scraping, copying, saving, or storing our data is strictly prohibited. A website that replicates or replaces the essential user experience of dribbble.com is prohibited. Competing designer search or hiring services are prohibited. You must remove from your application, within 24 hours, any Dribbble shot or personal information that the owner, or Dribbble, asks you to remove.
V619.relevance: Dribbble API ToS: explicit prohibitions on scraping, competing hiring services

V620.title: Medium API Documentation — Medium/GitHub
V620.author: Medium
V620.excerpt: Medium's API is a JSON-based OAuth2 API with all requests made to endpoints beginning at https://api.medium.com/v1. Browser-based OAuth authentication is supported for existing integrations only. For OAuth2, request access to credentials by emailing [email protected].
V620.relevance: Official Medium API status: OAuth2, write-focused, no public read API for other users

V621.title: Medium API (Unofficial) — MediumAPI.com
V621.author: MediumAPI.com
V621.excerpt: Unofficial API providing endpoints for fetching user articles, tags, followers, and publications from Medium. Uses Medium's undocumented internal web API. Risk: ToS violation potential similar to Substack's unofficial API.
V621.relevance: Existence of unofficial Medium API; risk assessment for using it
V621.credibility: medium — third-party unofficial service; ToS risk assessment is accurate

V622.title: Authorization — Notion API
V622.author: Notion
V622.excerpt: Public connections use OAuth 2.0 for authentication. Users select specific pages and databases to share with the integration during authorization, and the integration does not automatically gain access to all content in the workspace. The latest Notion API version is 2025-09-03.
V622.relevance: Notion API authorization model: page-scoped consent, not workspace-wide access

V623.title: Can You Fetch Content from Public Notion Pages Using the Official API Without OAuth? — Latenode Community
V623.author: Latenode Community
V623.excerpt: The official Notion API does not provide a way to access public Notion pages without OAuth, and attempting to access public pages that aren't shared with an integration results in a 404 error.
V623.relevance: Confirms Notion API returns 404 for public pages not shared via OAuth
V623.credibility: medium — community forum; consistent with observed API behavior

V624.title: Why Mirror.xyz Is the Future of Web3 Publishing — Eye of Unity NFT Foundation
V624.author: Eye of Unity NFT Foundation
V624.excerpt: Mirror is a web3 publishing platform similar to Medium, but relying on blockchain based encryption. Publishing is free, and only requires an Ethereum wallet. All of Mirror's posts are available to access on Arweave, an on-chain decentralized storage layer that makes it so that your posts exist forever.
V624.relevance: Mirror.xyz architecture: blockchain storage on Arweave; genuinely public data
V624.credibility: medium — Web3 community publication; accurately describes Mirror's architecture

V625.title: Basement Launches the Fastest API for Web3 Data — Basement/Mirror
V625.author: Basement
V625.excerpt: Basement API offers fast access to a wide range of on-chain data. Web3 data is by design publicly accessible on the blockchain — reading this data is inherently public access, unlike traditional platform scraping.
V625.relevance: Web3 data API access: public blockchain reads have different legal status than web scraping
V625.credibility: medium — company blog; accurate description of blockchain data accessibility

V626.title: What Recent Rulings in 'hiQ v. LinkedIn' and Other Cases Say About the Legality of Data Scraping — FBM Law
V626.author: FBM Law
V626.excerpt: Meta v. Bright Data (2024): This is a landmark win for public data scraping. X Corp v. Bright Data (2024): Twitter (now X) lost a similar lawsuit, reinforcing the same principle: scraping publicly accessible data without logging in is not a ToS violation, because the scraper never agreed to those terms.
V626.relevance: Case law on legality of scraping public websites: Meta v. Bright Data 2024 ruling
V626.credibility: high — law firm analysis of actual court rulings

V627.title: Court Rules in Favor of Bright Data in Meta v. Bright Data Case — Bright Data
V627.author: Bright Data
V627.excerpt: Judge Edward Chen granted summary judgment in favor of Bright Data. The judge ruled: 'The Facebook and Instagram Terms do not bar logged-off scraping of public data; perforce it does not prohibit the sale of such public data.' A survival clause purporting to prohibit scraping of public data in perpetuity was found unenforceable.
V627.relevance: Meta v. Bright Data Jan 2024: logged-out scraping of public data is not a ToS violation
V627.credibility: medium — from Bright Data (interested party); quotes are accurate; ruling is verifiable

V628.title: Is Web Scraping Illegal? Understanding the Legal Implications — Thunderbit
V628.author: Thunderbit
V628.excerpt: Scraping public web data is legal in the US and EU, provided you respect the CFAA, GDPR, a site's robots.txt, and reasonable rate limits. Robots.txt isn't legally binding, but it's best practice to respect it. Ignoring it signals bad faith.
V628.relevance: Current legal status of personal website scraping; robots.txt best practices
V628.credibility: medium — legal guidance from technology company; consistent with case law

V629.title: Art. 22 GDPR — Automated Individual Decision-Making, Including Profiling — GDPR-Info.eu
V629.author: European Union
V629.excerpt: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her. When decisions are based on consent: controller must implement suitable measures including the right to obtain human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.
V629.relevance: GDPR Art. 22 full text: automated decision-making restrictions applying to AI career insights
V629.credibility: high — authoritative GDPR text repository with Commission linkage

V630.title: Meta's €1.2 Billion GDPR Fine: Why It Still Matters in 2025 — GDPR Local
V630.author: GDPR Local
V630.excerpt: In May 2023, the Irish Data Protection Commission imposed a historic fine of €1.2 billion on Meta for transferring personal data of European users to the United States without adequate data protection mechanisms. This represents the biggest GDPR fine to date.
V630.relevance: Largest GDPR fine in history: Meta €1.2B May 2023 for EU-US data transfers
V630.credibility: high — GDPR specialist publication; fine is documented fact

V631.title: 20 Biggest GDPR Fines So Far [2025] — Data Privacy Manager
V631.author: Data Privacy Manager
V631.excerpt: The third biggest fine was for TikTok, who processed children's data improperly. TikTok received a €345 million fine in September 2023. In September 2024, the Irish Data Protection Commission fined Meta Ireland €91 million after passwords of social media users were stored in plaintext. Cumulative GDPR fines reached approximately €5.88 billion by January 2025.
V631.relevance: GDPR fine history: TikTok €345M (2023), Meta €91M (2024), cumulative €5.88B
V631.credibility: high — privacy management specialist; documents are verifiable

V632.title: Data Residency Requirements: EU vs US Explained — Secure Privacy
V632.author: Secure Privacy
V632.excerpt: GDPR does not mandate data localization within the EU. Instead, it requires that personal data transferred outside the EEA be protected by a legal mechanism — adequacy decision, SCCs, or BCRs — that ensures essentially equivalent protection. The EU-US Data Privacy Framework (July 2023) restored a legal mechanism for US-EU data transfers after Schrems II invalidated the Privacy Shield.
V632.relevance: GDPR data localization requirements and transfer mechanisms including EU-US DPF
V632.credibility: high — privacy technology company with legal expertise

V633.title: Rights Related to Automated Decision Making Including Profiling — ICO
V633.author: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO), UK
V633.excerpt: Article 22 applies only when there is no meaningful human involvement in the decision-making process. The CJEU confirmed in SCHUFA (December 2023) that even credit scoring algorithms fall under Article 22 when they significantly influence downstream human decisions.
V633.relevance: ICO guidance on Art. 22 + SCHUFA ruling: AI career scores visible to employers trigger Art. 22
V633.credibility: high — UK data protection regulator official guidance

V634.title: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) — California Office of the Attorney General
V634.author: California Office of the Attorney General
V634.excerpt: The CCPA permits businesses to sell personal information unless consumers opt out. Sale includes sharing personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising. Businesses cannot require account creation to process opt-out requests. Sensitive personal information categories include genetic data, biometric data, health information — professional employment information is NOT listed as sensitive.
V634.relevance: Official California CCPA source: sale definition, sensitive PI categories, opt-out requirements
V634.credibility: high — official California state government source

V635.title: CPRA Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information — Securiti
V635.author: Securiti
V635.excerpt: CPRA also sunsetted temporary exemptions for employee and B2B data. As of January 1, 2023, CPRA applies to personal information collected in the context of B2B communications. Currently, California residents (including those acting in a business capacity) have full privacy rights under the law.
V635.relevance: CPRA B2B and employee data exemption expiry: fully covered since January 1, 2023
V635.credibility: high — privacy technology and legal compliance company

V636.title: California Consumer Privacy Act's Employee and B2B Exemptions Expire January 1, 2023 — Stinson LLP
V636.author: Stinson LLP
V636.excerpt: The exemptions for employment-related personal information and personal information reflecting business-to-business transactions described in Civil Code Sec. 1798.145(m)-(n) expired on December 31, 2022. This change gives the covered categories of individuals the right to know what personal information is collected, request correction, delete personal information and request the limit of the use of sensitive personal information.
V636.relevance: CCPA/CPRA B2B and employment data exemption expiry confirmed by law firm
V636.credibility: high — established law firm analysis of statutory change

V637.title: Annex III: High-Risk AI Systems Referred to in Article 6(2) — EU Artificial Intelligence Act
V637.author: European Union
V637.excerpt: Section 4 (Employment, workers management): (a) AI systems intended to be used for the recruitment or selection of natural persons, in particular to place targeted job advertisements, to analyse and filter job applications, and to evaluate candidates. (b) AI systems intended to be used to make decisions affecting terms of work-related relationships, the promotion or termination of work-related contractual relationships.
V637.relevance: EU AI Act Annex III Section 4: employment and recruitment AI explicitly classified as high-risk
V637.credibility: high — authoritative EU AI Act text published on official act analysis site with Commission linkage

V638.title: What the EU AI Act Means for Staffing Businesses — EU Artificial Intelligence Act
V638.author: EU AI Act Resource Centre
V638.excerpt: The main date for staffing businesses is 2 August 2026, when the full suite of high-risk system obligations becomes enforceable for Annex III systems, including all employment-related AI. Every high-risk AI system must be used in a way that allows effective human oversight, and no AI tool should make final placement, rejection, or evaluation decisions without a qualified human in the loop.
V638.relevance: EU AI Act enforcement date for staffing/recruitment AI: August 2, 2026
V638.credibility: high — specialist EU AI Act analysis resource

V639.title: The EU's August 2, 2026 AI Act Deadline: Practical Obligations for High-Risk AI Systems — NetGuardia
V639.author: NetGuardia
V639.excerpt: For deployers who fail to meet their high-risk system obligations, fines can reach up to EUR 15 million or 3% of global annual turnover, whichever is higher. Article 26(7) requires you to inform workers' representatives and affected workers, extending in staffing to candidates and contingent workers, who have a right to know that AI is being used.
V639.relevance: EU AI Act fines for high-risk system non-compliance and Art. 26(7) transparency requirements
V639.credibility: high — cybersecurity and regulatory compliance specialist

V640.title: EU AI Act and Hiring: 2026 Compliance Guide — Truffle
V640.author: Truffle
V640.excerpt: Starting 2 August 2026, each employment AI tool will need mandatory risk assessments, technical documentation, bias testing, human oversight, transparency disclosures, and continuous monitoring. Deployers must keep logs generated by high-risk AI systems for at least six months.
V640.relevance: EU AI Act compliance requirements for employment AI: risk assessment, bias testing, 6-month logs

V641.title: China Relaxes Requirements for Cross-Border Data Transfers — Greenberg Traurig
V641.author: Greenberg Traurig LLP
V641.excerpt: On March 22, 2024, the Cybersecurity Administration of China (CAC) published the Provisions on Promoting and Regulating the Cross-border Flow of Data. The new regulations introduce exemptions including: cross-border shopping, HR management, and transfers involving fewer than 100,000 individuals.
V641.relevance: China PIPL 2024 cross-border transfer relaxation: HR management exemption + 100K threshold
V641.credibility: high — major US law firm analysis of regulatory change

V642.title: End of Grace Period: Brazil's Standard Contractual Clauses in International Transfers — Mayer Brown
V642.author: Mayer Brown LLP
V642.excerpt: The grace period granted for incorporating the SCCs to regularize international data transfers ended on August 23, 2025, one year after the publication of Resolution CD/ANPD No. 19/2024. From this point on, international data transfers will only be valid if the SCCs are implemented.
V642.relevance: Brazil LGPD mandatory SCCs for international data transfers: grace period ended August 23, 2025
V642.credibility: high — major international law firm; documents regulatory deadline

V643.title: Employee Data Privacy Laws by State: Your 2025 Guide — Acciyo
V643.author: Acciyo
V643.excerpt: Over a dozen states have enacted comprehensive data privacy laws, but for employees outside of California: HR records and employee data are almost completely exempt from these major laws. The UCPA, VCDPA, and CPA all explicitly exclude individuals 'acting in an employment or commercial context' from the definition of 'consumer.'
V643.relevance: US state privacy laws: employment data exemptions in VA, CO, CT, UT vs. California's full coverage
V643.credibility: medium — HR technology publication; accurate summary of state law provisions

V644.title: Data Localization and the Sovereign Cloud: EU Cloud Regulations Explained — Orrick
V644.author: Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP
V644.excerpt: GDPR does not require data localization within the EU. Organizations use Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) as the most widely used mechanism. When SCCs are used for transfers to countries without adequacy, a Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) is also required — an analysis of whether the laws of the receiving country would undermine the protection the SCCs purport to provide.
V644.relevance: GDPR SCCs and TIA requirements for EU-US data transfers

V645.title: US State Privacy Laws Comparison Chart (2026) — Recording Law
V645.author: Recording Law
V645.excerpt: As of May 2026, 22 US states have enacted comprehensive consumer privacy laws — Oklahoma (SB 546, signed March 20, 2026) and Alabama (HB 351, signed April 17, 2026) are the two most recent additions, with January 1, 2027 and May 1, 2027 effective dates respectively.
V645.relevance: Current count of US states with privacy laws: 22 states as of May 2026
V645.credibility: medium — legal information aggregator; current as of search date

V646.title: LinkedIn Recruiter Pricing in 2025: Full Cost Breakdown — Linked Helper
V646.author: Linked Helper
V646.excerpt: LinkedIn Recruiter Corporate costs approximately $10,800 per seat annually, while Recruiter Lite costs $1,680 for a single license. Pricing represents a nearly 15% increase from previous years. Response rates around 10 to 25%, and recruiters often need to send more messages.
V646.relevance: LinkedIn Recruiter pricing tiers 2025: $1,680-$10,800/seat/year; 15% price increase trend
V646.credibility: medium — LinkedIn automation tool company; pricing data consistent across multiple sources

V647.title: LinkedIn Recruiter Price Increase 2026: What Recruiting Agencies Are Doing — Leonar
V647.author: Leonar
V647.excerpt: LinkedIn has raised Recruiter Corporate pricing roughly 15% in 2026, with renewal invoices now landing at $10,800 to $12,960 per seat per year. The pushback, visible on LinkedIn posts and Reddit threads through Q1 and Q2 of 2026, is about the size of the increase. Many agencies absorbed a smaller mid-2025 price move already.
V647.relevance: LinkedIn Recruiter 2026 price increase and recruiter pushback/protest
V647.credibility: medium — recruiting software company; consistent with broader market reporting

V648.title: About Student Privacy in Handshake — Handshake Help Center
V648.author: Handshake
V648.excerpt: Students have complete control over what data they make public, and no student data uploaded to Handshake is visible to employers until the student chooses to make their profile public. Handshake provides three profile privacy options: Community, Employers, and Private. Personal email addresses will never be sold to any third party.
V648.relevance: Handshake's student privacy model: user-controlled visibility; data not sold to third parties

V649.title: US Officials Question 23andMe's Ability to Navigate Data Protection During Bankruptcy — IAPP
V649.author: IAPP
V649.excerpt: A bipartisan coalition of 28 attorneys general argued the company should not be able to sell users' biometric data during the acquisition. Twenty-eight states initially sought to block 23andMe's bankruptcy sale on this basis, arguing in part that genetic privacy laws prevent the sale of genetic information without express consumer consent.
V649.relevance: 23andMe bankruptcy: 28 AGs tried to block genetic data sale; court approved it anyway
V649.credibility: high — International Association of Privacy Professionals; authoritative privacy news

V650.title: 23andMe Bankruptcy: The Privacy Ombudsman's Report — Loeb & Loeb LLP
V650.author: Loeb & Loeb LLP
V650.excerpt: After declaring bankruptcy in March 2025, 23andMe sold the data of more than 15 million people around the world to TTAM Research Institute, a nonprofit organization created by 23andMe's founder. The Consumer Privacy Ombudsman found that because the data includes genetic information, which is inherently identifiable and incredibly sensitive, the sale poses a privacy risk not addressed by existing laws.
V650.relevance: 23andMe bankruptcy data sale: 15M users' genetic data sold to TTAM; privacy ombudsman findings
V650.credibility: high — major law firm analysis of court-appointed ombudsman report

V651.title: Privacy, Consent, and National Security After the 23andMe Bankruptcy — Lawfare
V651.author: Lawfare
V651.excerpt: Users who consented in 2017-2018 consented to 23andMe's privacy policy at the time. They did not consent to their genetic data being sold to a nonprofit in bankruptcy proceedings in 2025. This case demonstrates that consent at time of account creation is not sufficient when the business model changes or the company changes ownership.
V651.relevance: 23andMe key lesson: initial consent does not survive company acquisition/bankruptcy
V651.credibility: high — Lawfare is a respected national security/law publication

V652.title: The Art and Science Behind Spotify Wrapped — Spotify Newsroom
V652.author: Molly Holder, Spotify Senior Director of Personalization
V652.excerpt: Spotify personalization is a mix of giving you what you like while pushing you a bit outside your comfort zone. The platform tracks ways you interact with Spotify including playlist additions, complete song plays, skips, and artist engagement. In 2024, Spotify launched AI-generated personalized podcasts via Google NotebookLM.
V652.relevance: Spotify Wrapped engineering design: data signals, personalization approach, 2024 AI additions
V652.credibility: high — official Spotify newsroom; authored by Spotify personalization director

V653.title: Exploring the Animation Landscape of 2023 Wrapped — Spotify Engineering Blog
V653.author: Spotify Engineering
V653.excerpt: Behind every Wrapped recap is a robust architecture that processes petabytes of data with precision and speed. Apache Spark and other big data frameworks process this data at scale, and the results are stored in data warehouses, such as Google BigQuery. Data visualization tools aggregate this processed data, delivering personalized summaries to users in real time through APIs.
V653.relevance: Spotify Wrapped technical architecture: Apache Spark, BigQuery, real-time API delivery

V654.title: Your Year in Sport — Strava Support
V654.author: Strava
V654.excerpt: The Year In Sport summary is a highly personalized recap for Strava users highlighting unique data insights, meaningful social engagements, and stand-out moments. It includes metrics such as total distance covered, elevation gain, and insights into time spent moving. The Year in Sport section includes an added layer of storytelling unlocked via subscription.
V654.relevance: Strava Year in Sport: personalized metrics, episodic highlights, subscription-gated

V655.title: GitHub Wrapped 2025 — Your Coding Year in Review
V655.author: Git-Wrapped Community Project
V655.excerpt: GitHub Wrapped is like Spotify Wrapped but for GitHub activity. The tool presents your top languages, commit streaks, and more in an engaging story format. Generates a personalized summary of GitHub activity throughout the year and showcases repositories contributed to, issues resolved, and pull requests merged.
V655.relevance: GitHub Wrapped design: commit streaks, language breakdown, top project highlights
V655.credibility: medium — community-built third-party tool; demonstrates what GitHub API data yields

V656.title: Barnum Effect — The Decision Lab
V656.author: The Decision Lab
V656.excerpt: The Barnum effect is a type of cognitive bias that involves a tendency to believe that vague, general personality descriptions apply specifically and uniquely to them. We try to find relationships and patterns in the world around us, including trying to attach personal meaning to things that apply to many others.
V656.relevance: Barnum effect definition: why generic AI career insights feel personal but are not specific

V657.title: The Sophisticated Barnum Effect: How AI Became the Ultimate Yes-Man — Medium
V657.author: Thel Gunter
V657.excerpt: In the sophisticated Barnum effect, AI projects specificity which you accept as validation, and the AI fills in the blanks with plausible-sounding specifics that happen to agree with you. This works because AI is optimized for helpfulness, not truth-seeking.
V657.relevance: AI-specific Barnum effect: LLMs produce agreeable but non-specific 'insights'
V657.credibility: medium — individual author on Medium; makes a well-reasoned argument consistent with AI research

V658.title: The Barnum Effect: Why We're Enchanted by Tarot, Astrology, and Myers-Briggs — Choice Hacking
V658.author: Choice Hacking
V658.excerpt: Fill personality descriptions with vague, generic, and positive statements while avoiding any quantitative measures that concretely distinguish people from each other. To prevent falling for generic insights: seek out detailed information, consider whether the statement could apply to anyone else, question the source.
V658.relevance: Barnum effect design principles: vague positive statements vs. falsifiable specific claims
V658.credibility: medium — behavioral science applied to UX design; well-sourced

V659.title: Spotify Engineering Blog
V659.author: Spotify Engineering
V659.excerpt: Spotify partnered with Google AI's NotebookLM to create AI-generated personalized podcasts for Wrapped 2024 — the first major consumer application of generative AI for personalized insight narration at scale for 7+ million users simultaneously.
V659.relevance: Spotify 2024 Wrapped AI podcast: first major consumer generative AI personalization at scale

V660.title: LinkedIn API: Ultimate Guide to Integration, Pricing & Access (2026) — Phyllo
V660.author: Phyllo
V660.excerpt: LinkedIn is the most restrictive of all major social platforms when it comes to API access. Most endpoints require approval, and the free tier gives you very little. LinkedIn tokens expire after 60 days. The Talent Solutions API requires a partnership agreement and is not accessible to startups without a LinkedIn contract.
V660.relevance: Comprehensive LinkedIn API access assessment: most restrictive major platform
V660.credibility: high — API integration specialist company; consistent with official LinkedIn documentation

V661.title: LinkedIn API in 2026: Access, Endpoints, Limits & Alternatives — ConnectSafely
V661.author: ConnectSafely
V661.excerpt: Sign-In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect is available to all developers without approval. Beyond that, almost everything requires explicit LinkedIn review and approval. Alternatives to LinkedIn API: user uploads their own LinkedIn data export, or use third-party data enrichment (with legal risk).
V661.relevance: Confirms LinkedIn API alternative: user-initiated data export as compliant approach
V661.credibility: medium — technology guidance site; consistent with official LinkedIn documentation

V662.title: Start Building with the Notion API — Notion Developer Documentation
V662.author: Notion
V662.excerpt: Every connection or token has a set of capabilities that control what it can do — read content, update content, insert content, read comments. Public integrations follow the OAuth 2.0 protocol, which allows workspace members to give access to Notion pages directly through the auth flow. The latest Notion API version is 2025-09-03.
V662.relevance: Notion API capabilities overview: OAuth 2.0 public integrations, capability model
